behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names all file paths (CLAUDE.md, .claude/commands/ci-failures.md, .gitignore, .alexignore), all literal strings checked by tests (e.g., '# Next.js Development Guide', 'gt create', 'gt submit --no-edit', 'Graphite Stack Safety Rules', 'Never use `git stash` with Graphite', 'pnpm test-dev-turbo', 'pnpm test-start-turbo', 'pnpm test-unit', '# Check CI Failures', 'gh api', 'in_progress', 'subagent'/'Parallel subagent', '.claude/settings.local.json', '.claude/plans/', '.claude/todos.j

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction essentially IS the solution — it specifies the exact content to create in each file, including exact section headers, exact commands, exact text strings. There is no reasoning required; the agent just needs to transcribe what's written. For example, it says 'Create a section containing this exact text: Never use `git stash` with Graphite' and lists every command verbatim.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests like test_claude_md_not_empty assert len(content) > 1000 and test_ci_failures_md_not_empty asserts len(content) > 2000 and content.count('```') >= 4. These are specific to the gold solution's verbosity. A correct alternative that includes all required sections but is more concise could fail these length/count checks. Similarly, test_ci_failures_md_not_empty requires '```bash' in content and >=4 code blocks, which are implementation details not strictly necessary for correctness.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Nearly every fail-to-pass test is pure text matching: assert '# Next.js Development Guide' in content, assert 'gt create' in content, etc. The only behavioral tests are pass-to-pass tests (git status, prettier check, alex lint) which test the base repo state, not the agent's work product. All core f2p tests are 'assert string in open(path).read()' pattern.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub/empty file would fail because tests check for specific string content (e.g., 'gt submit --no-edit', 'Graphite Stack Safety Rules', etc.) and minimum length thresholds (>1000 chars, >2000 chars). An empty or minimal stub cannot pass these checks.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/next.js/../solution/solve.sh (or wherever Harbor mounts it). The agent could potentially read /solution/solve.sh which contains the complete gold patch with all file contents. Additionally, the test file at tests/test_outputs.py contains every literal string being checked, so grepping it would reveal exactly what strings to include.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All explicit requirements in instruction.md (CLAUDE.md content, ci-failures.md content, .gitignore entries, .alexignore entries, Graphite safety rules, testing commands) are tested by corresponding test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized into clear sections (fail-to-pass pr_diff, pass-to-pass repo_tests, pass-to-pass static) with descriptive function names and docstrings explaining what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task produces markdown configuration files, not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names across instruction.md and test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies exact content to write — exact section headers, exact commands, exact warning text. For a configuration file creation task, this goes beyond describing requirements and essentially dictates the solution line by line.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold diff creates new files (CLAUDE.md, .claude/commands/ci-failures.md) and appends entries to existing files (.gitignore, .alexignore) — all localized changes that don't contradict other files.
